other_observers: Many, including Steve Mlodinow who found the bird. Others included Doug Faulkner, Jim & Karen Schmoker, Maggie Boswell, Peter Gent, David Waltman, John Vanderpoel, Tim Smart, Gene Rutherford, et al.;
description: Large tern (similar in size to nearby Franklin's Gulls) with mostly pale gray upperparts. Wingtips appeared to be unmarked, black. Legs black, proportionately kind of short. Head mostly white with limited black high on nape & short black crest. This black region did not appear to reach eye, which stood out separately in whitish face (a bit of smudging may have reached the eye but only subtly so.) Bill yellowish-orange, long and modestly heavy and with only slight decurvature.Behaviors: During my observation the bird rested on a low-lying muddy island just offshore, sometimes above water and sometimes standing in the shallow water. The bird alternated between sleeping and looking around. The island roost was shared by Franklin's, Ring-necked, and California Gulls, Great Blue Herons, American White Pelicans, and American Coots.Call: not heardPlumage: Basic
